title: Switch to Recommends container-selinux
body:
This way we can install buildah into a container without pulling in
SELinux policy packages.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
This will need RPM version conditionals in the .spec if `Recommends` is used, as I don't believe RPM in EL7 supports it (it's RPM 4.11). `Recommends` was added in RPM 4.12. For earlier versions you will need to leave it as `Requires`.

Additionally, I can't see `containers-common` here, or in EPEL:
https://cbs.centos.org/repos/virt7-container-common-candidate/x86_64/os/Packages/

So it will have trouble resolving dependencies until this is built.
Yes I guess so. (Although I hate dirtying up the spec file, that is just an example and not something we actually ship on RHEL7 or Centos7

Even if it is just an example, i'd take 'Requiring' a few extra packages over breaking EL7 builds if modifying the .spec isn't an option.

EL7 is where a lot of the existing Docker users exist (RHEL7, CentOS7, Scientific Linux 7, Oracle Linux 7). I'd argue they are the enterprise customers to convince that Buildah is a viable alternative by making access to it as easy as possible.

Another alternative is to include a Fedora and EL7 spec file, rather than including the logic in the same file.
@pixdrift @lsm5 Will my check work on CENTOS7?

@rhatdan, yep
```
# cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Linux release 7.5.1804 (Core) 
# grep rhel /etc/rpm/macros.dist
%rhel 7
```
What's the story with `containers-common`? Should that stay as `skopeo-containers` on EL7 until the `containers-common` package is built for EL7? (ie. logic moved into the same `if` block)

@fkluknav when you do the next skopeo build for rhel 7.5, could you please rename the skopeo-containers package to containers-common ? Once that change is done, please also rebuild that source for CentOS Virt SIG.
Is this a good time to ask if it can be changed so the naming is consistent? ie. currently selinux package is singular _container_ and the common package is plural _container**s**_. 😟 😄
ha, `container-` SGTM. @rhatdan wdyt?
I think it is too late.  Since it is already shipped in Fedora and Ubuntu?
@rhatdan you mean rename to singular `container-` ? Though it's shipped already, we can have the rename done for future builds and have it Provides: containers-common. sound good?

I actually think we should leave it plural since we have
github.com/containers/*
containers/image
containers/storage
containers/skopeo

So the real problem is that we ship container-selinux, perhaps we should change this name.
@rhatdan seems like your diff is on an older spec file. The latest spec file already has `containers-common` change along with your seccomp.json changes. PTAL and let me know..
don't mean to drag this on :\, but there's also `container-storage-setup`. And in other tools, there's `systemd-container` (if that matters), and we might need to include `container-diff` in Fedora.
@pixdrift @lsm5 @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+
